name: Security group allows unrestricted SSH access (0.0.0.0/0)
description: A security group has an inbound rule allowing SSH (TCP/22) access from any IP address (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0). This exposes instances to internet-wide SSH brute-force and exploitation attempts.
remediation: Restrict SSH access to known management CIDRs or a bastion host IP. Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager as an SSH replacement — it requires no open inbound ports. Apply NACLs as a secondary defence.
